ORDER SUSTAINING DEMURRER WITH LEAVE TO AMEND

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS MARTHA RODRIGUEZ AND PABLO RODRIGUEZ' MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT OF PLAINTIFF NAIFEH AZAR IS GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO ADDRESS IN GOOD FAITH ALL ISSUES RAISED IN THIS MOTION. NON-OPPOSITION FILED. AMENDED COMPLAINT TO BE FILED AND SERVED WITHIN 10 DAYS OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (501)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MARTHA RODRIGUEZ, PABLO RODRIGUEZ'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. THE DEMURRER WILL BE HEARD IN DEPARTMENT 501 ON NOVEMBER 15, 2018 AT 9:30AM. THAT DEPARTMENT HEARS ALL CASES INVOLVING ALLEGATIONS OF FAILURE TO PAY RENT FOR RESIDENTIAL OR COMMERCIAL PREMISES. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/HK)
ORDER OVERRULING DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT MARTHA RODRIGUEZ, PABLO RODRIGUEZ DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED, 10 DAYS TO ANSWER. NOTHING ON THE FACE OF THE COMPLAINT OR JUDICIALLY NOTICEABLE DOCUMENTS INDICATE THAT CO-OWNERS OF THE PROPERTY ARE NECESSARY (OR INDISPENSABLE) PARTIES TO THIS ACTION. DEFENDANT FAILS TO CITE ANY AUTHORITY FOR THE PROPOSITIONS THAT NON-SIGNATORY CO-OWNERS ARE ENTITLED TO ENFORCE THE CONTRACT SEEKING UNPAID RENT AS A MATTER OF LAW UNDER ALL FACTUAL CIRCUMSTANCES. DEFENDANT FURTHER FAILS TO CITE ANY AUTHORITY FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT PLAINTIFF HAS TO INCLUDE THE ALLEGATIONS REGARDING POTENTIALLY NECESSARY PARTIES IN THE COMPLAINT. WHETHER THE CO-OWNERS ARE NECESSARY PARTIES IS AN ISSUE OF FACT THAT CANNOT BE DETERMINED AT THIS STAGE OF THE LITIGATION. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (501)
CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT (TRANSACTION ID # 100043273) FILED BY DEFENDANT RODRIGUEZ, MARTHA RODRIGUEZ, PABLO JURY DEMANDED, ESTIMATED TIME FOR TRIAL: 5.0 DAYS

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS MARTHA RODRIGUEZ, PABLO RODRIGUEZ'S MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING JOINDER OF PARTY IS DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING JOINDER OF PARTY IS DENIED. DEFENDANTS HAVE FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE THAT CO-OWNERS ARE NECESSARY (OR INDISPENSABLE) PARTIES TO THE ACTION UNDER CCP 389. ACCORDINGLY, ON THE FACTS PRESENTED, DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT SHOWN A "SUBSTANTIAL RISK" OF MULTIPLE OR INCONSISTENT LIABILITY. (UNION CARBIDE CORP. V. SUPERIOR COURT (1984) 36 CAL.3D 15, 21). DEFENDANTS CITE NO AUTHORITY TO SUPPORT JOINDER UNDER CCP 378, 379, OR 382. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Joinder Of Party
Off Calendar Jun-04-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Cary Gold, 415.947.0797 x2110 of Eviction Defense Collaborative for Defendant Martha Rodriguez.
Daniel Bornstein, 415.409.7611 of Bornstein Law for Plaintiff Naifeh Azar.

Argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS MARTHA RODRIGUEZ, PABLO RODRIGUEZ'S Motion For Order Compelling Joinder Of Party is Defendants' Motion for Order Compelling Joinder of Party is DENIED. Defendants have failed to demonstrate that co-owners are necessary (or indispensable) parties to the action under CCP 389. Accordingly, on the facts presented, Defendants have not shown a "substantial risk" of multiple or inconsistent liability. (Union Carbide Corp. v. Superior Court (1984) 36 Cal.3d 15, 21). Defendants cite no authority to support joinder under CCP 378, 379, or 382. Order signed. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-16-2019 Continued To Mar-19-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-31-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Carolyn Gold, 415.977.0797 ext. 2110 for Defendants/Moving parties Martha and Pablo Rodriguez.
Stanley Riddell, 415.463.2349 for Plaintiff/Opposing party Naifeh Azar.

Argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT MARTHA RODRIGUEZ, PABLO RODRIGUEZ DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. Nothing on the face of the complaint or judicially noticeable documents indicate that co-owners of the property are necessary (or indispensable) parties to this action. Defendant fails to cite any authority for the propositions that non-signatory co-owners are entitled to enforce the contract seeking unpaid rent as a matter of law under all factual circumstances. Defendant further fails to cite any authority for the proposition that Plaintiff has to include the allegations regarding potentially necessary parties in the complaint. Whether the co-owners are necessary parties is an issue of fact that cannot be determined at this stage of the litigation. Order signed. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)
